# v0.68 — Emit source maps: the load-bearing foundation (debugging track, slice 1)

- **Track:** [debugging](../tracks/debugging.md), slice 1 — the one slice with real
  code; slices 2–4 (Node attach, workerd attach, extension wiring) are downstream of
  the maps this produces.
- **Realises ADR:** [0103](../decisions/0103-source-map-contract.md) — the source-map
  contract (line-level, statement-anchored; nearest-enclosing-statement gap rule;
  sibling `.ts.map` + `//# sourceMappingURL`; `names`; the skip boundary; emission
  gating).
- **Posture:** tooling / codegen. The emitted TypeScript is unchanged except for a
  trailing `//# sourceMappingURL` comment; the new artefact is a sibling `.ts.map`. No
  language surface, no runtime behaviour change.

## Why now / why this shape

Slice 0 settled the format against an observed spike; nothing about the contract is
still open. What remains is the work the track always called load-bearing: the
emitter is a one-way string pipe (`write_line` in `lower.rs:293`; ~150 write sites in
`emit.rs`) that discards the `Span` every AST node carries, and `write_output`
(`lib.rs:35`) writes each file's `.typescript` with no map sibling. Every later slice
and every future codegen feature inherits whatever threading we land here, so this is
the slice to get the *shape* right — a position-tracking sidecar on the existing
writer, not a re-architecture.

The shape is exactly what the track's *Internal architecture* section sketched:
thread the span that's already in hand to the writer, accumulate `(generated line →
source span)`, and emit a sibling. The emitter stays string-append.

## What this slice does

1. **A `SourceMapBuilder`** (new, in `bynk-emit/src/emitter/`) accumulating
   `(generated line, source span)` pairs and serialising to a source-map v3 document
   (VLQ `mappings`, `sources`, `sourcesContent`, `names`, `file`). Line-level: one
   segment per generated line at column 0, per ADR 0103 D1.
2. **A line-tracking emission sink + a current-source-span cursor on `LowerCtx`**
   (`emitter.rs`). The sink counts newlines as text is appended; the cursor names the
   source span the current lines belong to. Set the cursor at statement and
   declaration boundaries; every physical line inherits it — realising the
   nearest-enclosing-statement rule (D2) without threading a span through all ~150
   `writeln!` sites. `write_line` gains an explicit span parameter for the in-hand
   case (lets, the `?` expansion, match arms — the constructs the spike validated);
   the top-level declaration emitters (`emit_free_fn`, `emit_refined`, `emit_record`,
   `emit_sum`, `emit_service`, …) set the cursor to the declaration's head span as the
   fallback anchor. This follows the existing `assert_loc` precedent on `LowerCtx`
   (v0.59) for carrying source context through lowering.
3. **Per-file source text reaches emit time.** `emit_project` is not given the source
   string today; the `ParsedFile` carries it. Thread a prebuilt per-file line-index
   (one pass with `span::line_col`, `span.rs:41`) into the emit context so the builder
   resolves each byte-offset `Span` to `(line, col)` once, not O(n) per lookup.
4. **`CompiledFile` carries the map.** Add `source_map: Option<SourceMap>`
   (`project.rs`), built during emission. `write_output` (`lib.rs:35`) gains a pass:
   write the sibling `<file>.ts.map` and append the `//# sourceMappingURL=<file>.ts.map`
   trailer to the `.ts`. The shared writer behind `bynkc compile`/`test` and `bynk
   dev`'s in-process build, so maps reach every local consumer uniformly.
5. **The skip boundary is recorded, not yet wired.** Per D5, `runtime.ts` and the
   generated worker glue map to themselves; the `skipFiles`/`smartStep` *launch-config*
   wiring is slice 4. Slice 1 only ensures their generated lines self-map (they are
   not `.bynk`-sourced), so a later config can skip them.
6. **Golden coverage by decode, not VLQ blobs** (see DECISION B): a focused test
   module decodes the emitted map for a `?` fixture and a `match` fixture and asserts
   the source↔generated line pairs the spike fixed (e.g. the `?` guard line maps to
   its `let` statement; each match arm's `case`/binding/`return` map to the arm).

## Decisions

**[DECISION A — sign-off] Writer threading: cursor-on-context, not span-per-writeln.**
Fork: (a) a line-tracking sink plus a current-span cursor set at statement/declaration
boundaries, lines inheriting it; or (b) pass an explicit source span to every one of
the ~150 `writeln!`/`push_str` sites. **Recommendation: (a).** It is what ADR 0103 D2
(nearest *enclosing statement*) is *for* — synthetic and signature lines correctly
inherit their construct's span, which the spike showed coalesces away under a
source-map-aware stepper. (b) is a 150-site mechanical churn that buys granularity the
contract explicitly defers. `write_line` still takes an explicit span for the
in-hand statement case; the cursor is the fallback, not the only path.

**[DECISION B — sign-off] Maps are emitted unconditionally; goldens verify by decode,
and the `.ts` harness normalises the trailer.** ADR 0103 D6 makes per-file map +
trailer emission unconditional. That appends `//# sourceMappingURL` to **516**
`expected.ts` goldens. Fork: (a) commit the trailer to all 516 *and* commit 516
`expected.ts.map` VLQ blobs; or (b) emit unconditionally, but update the e2e harness
(`bynkc/tests/.../e2e.rs`) to normalise the trailer line out of the `.ts` byte
comparison, and verify maps via a dedicated *decode* test over a handful of new
`?`/`match`/declaration fixtures. **Recommendation: (b).** 516 committed VLQ blobs are
unreadable, churn on every emitter touch, and give a reviewer zero signal; a decode
test that asserts named source→generated line pairs is what ADR 0103's Consequences
calls for and is the only golden form a human can review. The trailer is deterministic
and identical across files, so normalising one line is safe.

**[DECISION C] `sourcesContent` is embedded in slice 1.** Per D6, dev/test maps embed
source (the `workerd` inspector often can't resolve a `.bynk` path on disk). Slice 1
targets the local writers (`bynkc compile`/`test`, `bynk dev`), so it embeds.
*Recommendation:* embed now; the release-build strip (omit `sourcesContent`, no bundle
chaining) is gated on the build profile and lands with the production-bundle path
(slice 3+), out of scope here. Slice 1's output is pre-bundle TS, not a deployed
Worker, so nothing ships to production yet.

**[DECISION D] `names` ships present-but-minimal.** D4 reserves the `names` array from
the start but permits a partial set in phase 1. *Recommendation:* slice 1 emits the
array (format locked) and may leave it empty or seed it with the obvious binding
identifiers; rich variable-name mapping — the thing that makes the variable pane read
`w` not `__r0` — is the known rough edge the bynk-native semantic layer (track Phase 2)
owns, not a slice-1 blocker. Stepping and breakpoints, slice 1's actual goal, don't
depend on it.

**[DECISION E — sign-off] Version — recommend minor v0.68.** Adds a tooling artefact
(source maps), no language change → minor. **One thing to confirm:** the workspace
`Cargo.toml` is at `0.66.0` and the latest tag is `v0.66.0`, yet the v0.67
test-discovery increment already claimed the `v0.67` number in its commit. So either
v0.67's bump is still pending and this should reconcile it, or this is cleanly v0.68
over an unbumped tree. Recommend naming it **v0.68** and letting the implementation PR
run `scripts/bump-version.sh` to whatever lands consistent with v0.67 — flagged here
because the number is in the filename and the changelog.

## Risks & mitigations

- **Threading the cursor wrong drifts stepping.** A generated line that inherits a
  stale cursor maps to the wrong statement. Mitigated by the decode goldens pinning the
  exact pairs the spike fixed, including the lowered constructs where drift would show.
- **Statement-significant text emitted outside `write_line`** (declaration signatures,
  the `switch`/`case` lines, the `?` guard in `lower.rs`) maps to the enclosing-cursor
  span by default. Usually right (D2); a construct that lowers to genuinely *out-of-line*
  code (a hoisted helper) would mis-anchor. Mitigated: none exist today (lowering is
  contiguous — the spike confirmed it); if one is added later it is an amendment to
  D2, and the decode test is the guard that catches it.
- **`line_col` cost.** Resolving every span via `span.rs:41` is O(offset) per call.
  Mitigated by building one line-index per file and reusing it (item 3), not calling
  `line_col` per mapping.
- **Map composition through the bundler is unverified** (open question 3). Out of scope
  — slice 1 realises only the §19 *debugger* half; the production stack-trace half waits
  on slice 3's `wrangler`/esbuild confirmation. Stated so no one reads slice 1 as
  closing it.

## Spec & docs delta

- **Spec:** none. ADR 0103 is the normative record of the map contract; the language
  spec is untouched (no surface change).
- **Docs (required, per the proposals README):**
  - `docs/src/reference/changelog.md` — a v0.68 row: emitted output now carries
    source maps (`.ts.map` siblings + trailer); the foundation for step debugging.
  - The compilation/output reference page (wherever emitted-artefact layout is
    described) gains a line noting the `.ts.map` sibling and that `.bynk` source is not
    shipped to deployed Workers by default (forward-ref ADR 0103 D6).
  - Advance the "written against vX.Y" currency banner + `spec/appendix-version-history.md`.
  - **Deliberately deferred:** no "Debugging" guide/how-to page yet — there is no
    attach UX until slices 2–4. Writing a debugging guide now would document a feature a
    user can't run. The changelog frames v0.68 as the *foundation*, not the feature.

## Done when

- `SourceMapBuilder` emits a valid source-map v3 document; `write_output` writes the
  sibling `.ts.map` and the `//# sourceMappingURL` trailer for every emitted `.ts`.
- Spans thread through `write_line` and the declaration emitters via the
  context cursor; the per-file line-index reaches emit time.
- Decode goldens assert source↔generated line pairs for a `?` fixture, a `match`
  fixture, and a top-level declaration — including the gap cases (guard line, arm
  lines) anchored per D2.
- The e2e harness normalises the trailer; all 516 existing `.ts` goldens stay green
  with no VLQ-blob churn.
- `bynkc compile`, `bynkc test`, and `bynk dev` all emit maps (shared writer).
- Changelog + currency banner updated; no debugging guide page (explained above).
- Version bumped (`scripts/bump-version.sh`) per DECISION E.
- On merge: mark slice 1 ✅ in the track, add the dated decision-log entry, mark the
  §19 source-map commitment *realised* (the debugger half), and delete this proposal.
